roles.40595850: triggering alert: mimikatz.exe accessed lsass.exe with read access (GrantedAccess 0x1010) — credential dumping
roles.40591054: benign context: svchost.exe accessed lsass.exe (GrantedAccess 0x1400, lsm.dll CallTrace) — normal Local Session Manager access, no VM_READ
roles.40597812: rundll32.exe accessed lsass.exe (GrantedAccess 0x1410) with comsvcs.dll on the CallTrace — the comsvcs MiniDump technique
roles.40604410: xordump.exe accessed lsass.exe with full access (GrantedAccess 0x1fffff) — credential-dumping tool

metadata.security_proposition: The named dumping processes performed offensive memory access against LSASS.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG (named dumping tools mimikatz/comsvcs-rundll32/xordump access lsass with dump-capable GrantedAccess and comsvcs CallTrace; direction dumper->lsass, correct). Calibration role = PRESERVE (validator must NOT down-rank a genuine dump). Contrast: one benign svchost/lsm access (0x1400, no VM_READ). Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: Verified: every log in T1003.001/atomic_red_team is a DIFFERENT host (creddump=win-dc-137, security=win-dc-811, procdump-security=win-host-117, createdump-sysmon=win-host-622, main-sysmon=win-dc-262/807). No same-host fusion. Tier-1 single-sensor.

boundary_definition: EV = the comsvcs-rundll32 MiniDump and xordump full-access dumps, plus one benign svchost/lsm access (16:25) as a legitimate-accessor contrast.
This Sysmon capture is EID10 (process_access) ONLY — no process_create/file_create; the credential-reading ACCESS is itself the evidence.
EXCLUDED: duplicate EID10 accesses, the borderline wmiprvse/cimwin32 access, and repeated benign svchost accesses.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: A named credential-dumping tool (or dump-capable GrantedAccess + dumping CallTrace) accessing lsass.exe is the T1003.001 detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: mimikatz.exe reading lsass memory is the clearest triggering event; corroborated by comsvcs and xordump.

event_groups.description: mimikatz (0x1010), comsvcs-rundll32 (0x1410, comsvcs.dll), xordump (0x1fffff) -> lsass. Dump-capable access, correct direction.
svchost -> lsass (0x1400, lsm.dll) — legitimate LSM access, no VM_READ.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: STRONG / preserve. Multiple named dumping tools access LSASS with dump-capable masks and a comsvcs call trace in the correct source->target direction. This decisively establishes offensive credential-access behavior, although this EID10-only source does not show a dump file or recovered credentials.
0x1010/0x1410/0x1fffff include PROCESS_VM_READ (can read lsass memory) = dump-capable; 0x1400 (svchost) lacks VM_READ = benign query. This case exercises the LSASS GrantedAccess rule.
All accesses are SOURCE(tool)->TARGET(lsass) — the CORRECT credential-dumping direction (contrast PS-001's reversed lsass->powershell trap).
EID10-only capture; no command line or dump file. The dump-capable memory-read access is the evidence. A richer full-telemetry dump exists on other hosts but would break single-source.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
